action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 4 completed training (500 samples, 1 epoch) and submitted eval. The grounding_score is 0.0013 (2/1581 correct), which is essentially random. This suggests either a critical pipeline bug or severe underfitting. I need to diagnose whether the base model works, whether the merged checkpoint degrades performance, and inspect raw model outputs before scaling up training. Analysis: Running a head-to-head comparison of base vs. fine-tuned model on a handful of val samples will reveal if the fine-tuning broke the model or if predict.py parsing is flawed. Decision: Write a diagnostic script that loads both the base model and the merged checkpoint, runs inference on 20 val samples, prints raw generated text, and computes local grounding accuracy. This will pinpoint the issue in one iteration.
